source_notes: First export shipment Apr 1 1969 (Osumi Maru to Japanese steel mills). Initial JV included BHP and a Japanese equity request of 10% from steel-mill consortium. Current JV: BHP 85%, Mitsui-ITOCHU Iron 10%, ITOCHU Minerals & Energy Australia 5% (total Japanese 15%). Sources: Mitsui Australia; Business & Human Rights Resource Centre; Lexology 60-year overview.

japanese_partner: Mitsui Iron Ore Development + Cape Lambert Iron Associates (1972); Nippon Steel + Sumitomo Metal added 1977
source_notes: First shipment Oct 5 1972 (Fujisan Maru). Original JV: Robe River Ltd (Aus) 35%, Mitsui Iron Ore Development 30%, Cape Lambert Iron Associates (Japanese) 5%, Cliffs Western Australian Mining (US) 30%. Japanese equity in 1972 was 35% (30% Mitsui + 5% Cape Lambert). Nippon Steel + former Sumitomo Metal added equity participation in 1977. Current: Rio Tinto 53%, Mitsui & Co. 33%, Nippon Steel 14% = Japanese 47% total. Sources: Mininglink Robe River; Mitsui Iron Ore (Rio Tinto Operations) page; Rio Tinto 50-yr release; WA legislation file (1985 Cleveland-Cliffs Agreement).

Mine commenced 1971 under Central Queensland Coal Associates: Utah International 85% / Mitsubishi Development 15%. BHP acquired Utah's Australian assets in 1984; the 50:50 BHP-Mitsubishi Alliance (BMA) structure was only formed in 2001. Major coking-coal supplier to Japanese steel mills throughout. Source: NS Energy Business Goonyella Riverside profile.
Same CQCA-then-BMA history as Goonyella. Production began 1972. Initial Japanese equity 15% (Mitsubishi Development in CQCA); 50% from 2001 (BMA). Source: Mining Monthly BMA profile.
Production began 1974; same Utah/Mitsubishi to BHP/Mitsubishi history. Source: Wikipedia Saraji coal mine.

Production began 2003. Equity (pre-2018 Glencore acquisition): Rio Tinto 82%, Nippon Steel 8%, Marubeni Coal 6.67%, Sumisho Coal Development 3.33% = Japanese 18%. Source: Wikipedia / Glencore acquisition release 2018.

Production began 1999. Original JV: CNA 40%, Wesfarmers 40%, Taipower (Taiwan) 10%, Mitsui 10%. Mitsui exited in late 2018 when New Hope consolidated ownership. Source: Wesfarmers history; GEM Wiki Bengalla.

Modern open-cut mine commenced 1986 (prior small Ulan No. 1 Colliery dates to 1920s). Pre-2018 JV: Xstrata/Glencore 90%, Mitsubishi Development 10%. Mitsubishi sold its 10% stake to Glencore in 2018. Source: Mitsubishi Corp 2018-09 release; Glencore Ulan page.
